rag_rules: Keep component-type contract content in type scope, and keep implementation-level bindings in implementation scope.
Do not place implementation-only subcomponent or connection references inside a type-scoped annex.
When merging into an implementation, place the annex after the referenced structural declarations so scope is already established.
generic_rules: Fuse annex content into the owner whose visible symbols match the annex references.
Preserve the existing architecture exactly; merging annex text must not add or rename ports, subcomponents, connections, or properties.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Type scope normally carries assume/guarantee/const/eq content, while implementation scope carries implementation-only bindings such as assign.
anti_patterns: Do not move an annex into a broader scope if that would hide or invalidate the identifiers it uses.
Do not invent declarations or restructure the model to satisfy unresolved annex references during fusion.
irrelevant_topics: Property-as-variable diagnostic details unless annex text itself is being repaired
